results.note: All build commands confirmed in package.json: build:quick (tauri build --no-bundle), build:debug (tauri build --debug --no-bundle), tauri dev (via vite+tauri), tauri build (full). Portable exe paths correct. xterm.js 6.0 confirmed (@xterm/xterm ^6.0.0).
Policy entry — no code to verify. Build commands exist for quick/debug builds per BV-01.
Policy entry referencing toolchain checks. tsc, vitest, and cargo check are all available. package.json has test script (vitest run).
All IPC commands defined in commands.rs with #[tauri::command], registered in lib.rs via tauri::generate_handler![...]. 42 commands registered including commands:: and jsonl_watcher:: namespaces.

Types mostly mirror with camelCase (Rust uses #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]). However: (1) TS SessionState has 'actionNeeded' which Rust SessionState lacks — it's a frontend-only refinement derived in useInspectorState.ts. (2) Rust SessionMetadata has 5 fields not in TS (subagent_count, task_progress, context_warning, recent_output, subagent_activity). The types diverge intentionally but the claim 'mirror' is inaccurate.

Zustand stores in src/store/ (sessions.ts, settings.ts). Hooks in src/hooks/ (useTerminal.ts, usePty.ts, useInspectorState.ts, useCommandDiscovery.ts, useCliWatcher.ts, useNotifications.ts, useCtrlKey.ts). All confirmed.

All components follow <Name>/<Name>.tsx pattern with co-located CSS. 10 CSS files found matching their component directories. Icons component has no CSS (SVG-only, uses currentColor). All component directories verified.
Tests exist for lib/ functions (13 test files in src/lib/__tests__/) and store actions (sessions.test.ts in src/store/__tests__/). Policy entry — compliance is ongoing.

All async Rust commands that spawn subprocesses use tokio::task::spawn_blocking(). 13 spawn_blocking calls found across commands.rs. Sync helper functions (detect_claude_cli_sync, list_past_sessions_sync, etc.) are always called from within spawn_blocking closures.

All Windows subprocess Command::new calls include .creation_flags(0x08000000) behind #[cfg(target_os = "windows")] guards. 5 creation_flags calls found covering: where, claude --version, claude --help, wmic (kill_session_holder), wmic (kill_orphan_sessions). Non-Windows pgrep calls correctly omit this flag.

All colors are CSS custom properties on :root via applyTheme() in theme.ts, called at startup in main.tsx. Components use var(--name) in CSS — the only hardcoded hex found in CSS files are fallback values inside var() expressions (e.g., var(--warning, #c47a20)). Model rarity colors in claude.ts are fixed hex (#ff8000, #a335ee, #4e9bff) as documented exception. getXtermTheme() reads CSS variables confirmed.

All key variables confirmed set in applyTheme(): --bg-primary, --bg-surface, --accent (clay #d4744a), --accent-secondary (blue #6ea8e0), --accent-tertiary (purple #bc8cff), --term-bg, --term-fg. Colors match described roles.
